heading: Readmission requirements for servicemembers
label: 1091c Readmission requirements for servicemembers

children.heading: Definition of service in the uniformed services
children.label: a Definition of service in the uniformed services
children.text: In this section, the term “service in the uniformed services” means service (whether voluntary or involuntary) on active duty in the Armed Forces, including such service by a member of the National Guard or Reserve.

children.heading: Discrimination against students who serve in the uniformed services prohibited
children.label: b Discrimination against students who serve in the uniformed services prohibited
children.text: A person who is a member of, applies to be a member of, performs, has performed, applies to perform, or has an obligation to perform, service in the uniformed services shall not be denied readmission to an institution of higher education on the basis of that membership, application for membership, performance of service, application for service, or obligation.

children.label: A
children.text: the student (or an appropriate officer of the Armed Forces or official of the Department of Defense) gives advance written or verbal notice of such service to the appropriate official at the institution of higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: the cumulative length of the absence and of all previous absences from that institution of higher education by reason of service in the uniformed services does not exceed five years; and

children.label: C
children.text: except as otherwise provided in this section, the student submits a notification of intent to reenroll in the institution of higher education in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: a mission, operation, exercise, or requirement that is classified; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a pending or ongoing mission, operation, exercise, or requirement that may be compromised or otherwise adversely affected by public knowledge.

children.heading: Failure to give advance notice
children.label: B Failure to give advance notice
children.text: Any student (or an appropriate officer of the Armed Forces or official of the Department of Defense) who did not give advance written or verbal notice of service to the appropriate official at the institution of higher education in accordance with paragraph (1)(A) may meet the notice requirement by submitting, at the time the student seeks readmission, an attestation to the student’s institution of higher education that the student performed service in the uniformed services that necessitated the student’s absence from the institution of higher education.

children.label: A
children.text: that is required, beyond five years, to complete an initial period of obligated service;

children.label: B
children.text: during which such student was unable to obtain orders releasing such student from a period of service in the uniformed services before the expiration of such five-year period and such inability was through no fault of such student; or

children.label: i
children.text: ordered to or retained on active duty under section 688, 12301(a), 12301(g), 12302, 12304, or 12305 of title 10 or under section 251, 252, 359, 360, 367, or 712 of title 14;11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: ordered to or retained on active duty (other than for training) under any provision of law because of a war or national emergency declared by the President or the Congress, as determined by the Secretary concerned;

children.label: iii
children.text: ordered to active duty (other than for training) in support, as determined by the Secretary concerned, of an operational mission for which personnel have been ordered to active duty under ;section 12304 of title 10

children.label: iv
children.text: ordered to active duty in support, as determined by the Secretary concerned, of a critical mission or requirement of the Armed Forces (including the National Guard or Reserve); or

children.label: v
children.text: called into Federal service as a member of the National Guard under chapter 13 of title 10 or .section 12406 of title 10

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a student referred to in subsection (a) shall, upon the completion of a period of service in the uniformed services, notify the institution of higher education of the student’s intent to return to the institution not later than three years after the completion of the period of service.

children.heading: Hospitalization or convalescence
children.label: B Hospitalization or convalescence
children.text: A student who is hospitalized for or convalescing from an illness or injury incurred in or aggravated during the performance of service in the uniformed services shall notify the institution of higher education of the student’s intent to return to the institution not later than two years after the end of the period that is necessary for recovery from such illness or injury.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: C Special rule
children.text: A student who fails to apply for readmission within the period described in this section shall not automatically forfeit such eligibility for readmission to the institution of higher education, but shall be subject to the institution of higher education’s established leave of absence policy and general practices.

children.label: i
children.text: the student has not exceeded the service limitations established under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the student’s eligibility for readmission has not been terminated due to an exception in subsection (d).

children.heading: Prohibited documentation demands
children.label: B Prohibited documentation demands
children.text: An institution of higher education may not delay or attempt to avoid a readmission of a student under this section by demanding documentation that does not exist, or is not readily available, at the time of readmission.

children.heading: No change in academic status
children.label: 6 No change in academic status
children.text: A student who is readmitted to an institution of higher education under this section shall be readmitted with the same academic status as such student had when such student last attended the institution of higher education.
A separation of such person from the Armed Forces (including the National Guard and Reserves) with a dishonorable or bad conduct discharge.
A dismissal of such person permitted under .section 1161(a) of title 10
A dropping of such person from the rolls pursuant to .section 1161(b) of title 10
